title: impatiens tilo
intro: Impatiens tilo, also known as the "Balsam of Peru" or "Balsam of Tolu," is a flowering plant native to the Andean region. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in resin, which contains medicinal compounds such as salicylic acid, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, with its medicinal properties attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and ease pain. In herbal preparations, Impatiens tilo is often combined with other plants to create remedies for colds, coughs, and skin irritations, and can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
